summary: Verified expanded Terabox URL validation (12 new mirror hosts + urlparse-based lenient fallback). Created new focused test file /app/backend/tests/test_terabox_url_validation.py with 27 parametrized cases covering: (a) all 12 new mirror hosts pass validation and return 422 from upstream (not 400), (b) 5 previously-whitelisted hosts still pass (regression), (c) 7 non-terabox URLs correctly rejected with 400 including edge cases 'tera in path only' and '1024 without box', (d) 2 lenient-fallback cases (tera-in-host) accepted, (e) empty URL rejected. Full backend suite: 43/43 pass (16 existing regression + 27 new). Frontend hint chips visually confirmed via screenshot: 'terashare.co', 'teraboxshare.com', '+ all Terabox mirrors' now displayed. End-to-end UI verified: youtube.com URL -> 400 rejection toast; terashare.co URL -> 422 with correct 'Could not extract' toast (validation passed, upstream failed as expected).

verified_frontend.hero_input_flow_non_terabox: https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc -> 400 -> UI toast rejects with 'Not a valid Terabox link'
verified_frontend.hero_input_flow_new_mirror: https://terashare.co/s/1fakeurl -> 422 -> UI toast 'Could not extract this Terabox link. It may be private, expired, or unsupported.' (validation PASSED - critical fix confirmed)

verified_regression.test_linkplay_backend.py: 16/16 pass (auth, quota, subscription, favorites, history, continue-watching, google session invalid, webhook signature)
verified_regression.test_terabox_url_validation.py: 27/27 pass (12 new mirrors + 5 whitelisted regression + 7 rejection + 2 lenient fallback + 1 empty)

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is_valid_terabox_url() is well-structured: explicit whitelist first (fast path, O(n) substring), then urlparse-based lenient fallback that correctly checks hostname (not entire URL) - so 'https://foo.com/tera/file' is correctly rejected while 'https://terashare.co/...' is correctly accepted.

Note the existing substring-check in the whitelist path (`h in url_lower`) is a bit loose in theory (e.g., 'terabox.com' appearing in a path could match), but combined with URL validation and downstream extractor rejection it's acceptable. The urlparse fallback is the more rigorous branch.
Minor suggestion (non-blocking): the whitelist branch could also use urlparse for consistency, but the current dual approach is safe.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (chips visually verified + E2E UI validation matrix confirmed)

test_credentials: Fresh timestamped emails via conftest.py fixture. Password: password123.
seed_data_creation: None persisted beyond per-test fresh users (isolated).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Terabox URL validation change is production-safe. Canonical URL validation test file: /app/backend/tests/test_terabox_url_validation.py (27 cases). Full regression: /app/backend/tests/test_linkplay_backend.py (16 cases). Combined 43 tests run in ~22s against public preview URL.
